skill.name: fda-consultant-specialist
skill.description: Provides FDA regulatory strategy support for medical device teams, including pathway planning, QSR readiness, HIPAA assessment, cybersecurity preparation, and submission planning.
skill.summary: Plan FDA medical device submissions, compliance reviews, and regulatory readiness work.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and Ruby shell backtick patterns, but review found documentation-only content. The weak crypto hits are FDA regulatory terms or placeholder examples, and the backticks are Markdown code fences or filename formatting. No prompt injection, exfiltration pattern, or executable command instruction was found in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Regulatory Terms Flagged as Weak Crypto
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain FDA regulatory phrases, checklist text, or placeholder documentation, not cryptographic functions, hashing, encryption, or security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are plain regulatory or placeholder text. No cryptographic algorithm, API call, or implementation logic appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown formatting for diagrams and resource filenames, not Ruby execution syntax or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows fenced documentation blocks and inline filename references. There is no instruction to execute commands and no dynamic command construction.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams face complex FDA, QSR, HIPAA, and cybersecurity requirements. This skill helps structure regulatory strategy, readiness reviews, and submission planning with clear compliance-focused guidance.
actual_capabilities: Explains FDA medical device pathways such as 510(k), PMA, De Novo, and pre-submission planning.
Structures QSR compliance reviews around design controls, CAPA, document controls, and management responsibility.
Guides HIPAA risk assessment planning for devices and systems that handle protected health information.
Outlines FDA cybersecurity readiness topics, including SBOM, vulnerability handling, and post-market monitoring.
Helps prepare inspection readiness, warning letter response strategy, and regulatory communication plans.
limitations: Does not replace licensed legal, regulatory, clinical, or quality system advice.
Cannot submit materials to FDA or verify current FDA database records without external research.
May need current FDA guidance updates before use in high-stakes regulatory decisions.
Provides planning support, not final approval of compliance status or submission quality.

use_cases.title: Plan an FDA Pathway
use_cases.description: Compare likely submission routes and identify evidence needed before a formal regulatory strategy review.

use_cases.title: Review Quality System Readiness
use_cases.description: Organize a QSR gap assessment across design controls, CAPA, document control, and management review areas.

use_cases.title: Prepare Connected Device Compliance
use_cases.description: Map HIPAA and cybersecurity considerations for a connected medical device or SaMD product.

prompt_templates.title: Identify a Likely FDA Pathway
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this device concept and identify likely FDA pathway options. Include classification questions, predicate research needs, and key uncertainty areas.

prompt_templates.title: Create a QSR Gap Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a QSR readiness checklist for our medical device team. Cover design controls, CAPA, document controls, management responsibility, and inspection evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Assess HIPAA and Cybersecurity Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess HIPAA and FDA cybersecurity considerations for this connected medical device workflow. Identify PHI data flows, safeguards, SBOM needs, and open risks.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Regulatory Strategy Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a regulatory strategy brief for this device. Include pathway rationale, evidence plan, pre-submission questions, quality system dependencies, and risk mitigations.

output_examples.input: We are building a wearable monitor with clinical alerts. What should we evaluate first?
output_examples.output: The response would outline device classification questions, predicate search needs, intended use clarity, software risk, clinical evidence needs, and pre-submission planning topics.

output_examples.input: Our SaMD product stores patient readings in a cloud dashboard. What compliance areas need review?
output_examples.output: The response would summarize HIPAA data flow mapping, access controls, audit controls, transmission security, FDA software documentation, SBOM planning, and cybersecurity monitoring.

output_examples.input: We received FDA inspection observations. Help organize a response plan.
output_examples.output: The response would structure root cause analysis, CAPA actions, effectiveness checks, documentation updates, ownership, timelines, and professional FDA communication points.

best_practices: Provide device type, intended use, patient population, software role, and available evidence when asking for pathway guidance.
Ask the skill to separate confirmed facts, assumptions, open questions, and items requiring professional review.
Verify final regulatory decisions against current FDA guidance, official databases, and qualified counsel.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated guidance as final legal, regulatory, clinical, or quality approval.
Do not provide protected health information or confidential patient records in prompts.
Do not ask for a guaranteed FDA clearance outcome or a shortcut around required evidence.

faq.question: Can this skill determine my final FDA classification?
faq.answer: It can help analyze likely pathways and open questions, but final classification should be verified with current FDA resources and qualified experts.

faq.question: Does it support 510(k), PMA, and De Novo planning?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes these pathways and helps organize evidence, predicate research, and pre-submission planning.

faq.question: Can it review HIPAA risks for connected devices?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help map PHI flows, safeguards, access controls, transmission security, and documentation needs.

faq.question: Does it replace a regulatory consultant or attorney?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance and planning support, but high-stakes decisions require qualified professional review.

faq.question: Can Codex or Claude Code use this skill for documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help draft checklists, strategy briefs, gap assessments, and response outlines from provided context.

faq.question: What information should I include in a prompt?
faq.answer: Include intended use, device description, user workflow, software functions, data handling, evidence status, and known regulatory concerns.
